name: CP/M 2.2 Standard Symbols
description: Canonical names and conventions for Digital Research CP/M 2.2 (1981). Covers the BIOS jump table, BDOS function numbers, zero-page layout, FCB structure, and well-known constants. All entries are documented in the cited primary sources unless explicitly marked '(inferred)'.

sources.name: Digital Research CP/M 2.2 Operating System Manual (1979/1981)
CP/M 2.2 Alteration Guide (Section 6 — BIOS jump vector)
John Elliott — CP/M information archive: BIOS
John Elliott — CP/M information archive: BDOS function summary
John Elliott — CP/M information archive: BDOS system calls

page_zero._description: Standard CP/M 2.2 zero-page layout, $0000-$00FF. Shared between user programs (TPA) and CP/M itself; programs MUST preserve $0005-$0007 (BDOS vector) and read but not corrupt $0003-$0004 (IOBYTE, current drive).

0x0000.name: WBOOT_VEC
0x0000.comment: Warm-boot vector — JP WBOOT in BIOS. Touching it causes a CP/M warm boot.

0x0003.name: IOBYTE
0x0003.comment: I/O assignment byte — logical-to-physical device routing (CONSOLE/READER/PUNCH/LIST). 4 fields × 2 bits each.

0x0004.name: CDISK
0x0004.comment: Current drive (low nibble: 0=A, 1=B, ..., 15=P) and current user (high nibble, 0-15).

0x0005.name: BDOS_VEC
0x0005.comment: BDOS call vector — JP BDOS_ENTRY. Programs use CALL $0005 to invoke BDOS. Word at $0006 is also the top-of-TPA marker.

0x0008.name: RST1_VEC
0x0008.comment: Z-80 RST 1 ($08) restart vector — 8 bytes. Available for application/debugger use.

0x0010.name: RST2_VEC
0x0010.comment: Z-80 RST 2 ($10) restart vector — 8 bytes. Available for application/debugger use.

0x0018.name: RST3_VEC
0x0018.comment: Z-80 RST 3 ($18) restart vector — 8 bytes. Available for application/debugger use.

0x0020.name: RST4_VEC
0x0020.comment: Z-80 RST 4 ($20) restart vector — 8 bytes. Available for application/debugger use.

0x0028.name: RST5_VEC
0x0028.comment: Z-80 RST 5 ($28) restart vector — 8 bytes. Available for application/debugger use.

0x0030.name: RST6_VEC
0x0030.comment: Z-80 RST 6 ($30) restart vector — 8 bytes. Available for application/debugger use.

0x0038.name: RST7_VEC
0x0038.comment: Z-80 RST 7 ($38) restart vector — 8 bytes. Conventionally reserved for debuggers (DDT, ZSID).

0x0040.name: USER_AREA
0x0040.comment: $0040-$004F — 16 bytes available for system implementation use.

0x0050.name: RESERVED_50
0x0050.comment: $0050-$005B — reserved by CP/M; do not use.

0x005C.name: DEFAULT_FCB
0x005C.comment: Default File Control Block — populated by CCP from command-line argument 1. Standard 36-byte FCB structure (drive + filename + extents + record number).

0x006C.name: DEFAULT_FCB2
0x006C.comment: $006C-$007B — second filename area. Overlaps with the second half of DEFAULT_FCB. Populated by CCP from command-line argument 2.

0x007C.name: DEFAULT_CR
0x007C.comment: Default FCB current record byte (overlaps end of DEFAULT_FCB2).

0x007D.name: DEFAULT_RND
0x007D.comment: Default FCB random record number (3 bytes — low/middle/high).

0x0080.name: DEFAULT_DMA
0x0080.comment: Default DMA buffer — also command-line tail. First byte = length, then characters. Returned to default by BDOS function 13 (DRV_ALLRESET).

0x0080_alt.name: CMDLINE_LEN
0x0080_alt.comment: First byte of DEFAULT_DMA — length of command-line tail (0-127). Same address as DEFAULT_DMA.

0x0081.name: CMDLINE
0x0081.comment: Command-line tail characters (uppercase, with leading space). Same buffer as DEFAULT_DMA.

0x0100.name: TPA_START
0x0100.comment: Start of Transient Program Area. .COM files load here and execute starting at $0100.

bios_jump_table._description: Standard 17-entry CP/M 2.2 BIOS jump table at the BIOS base. Each entry is 3 bytes (JMP target). Keys are byte offsets from the BIOS base address. Some early SoftCard CP/M builds use only the first 15 entries (CP/M 2.0 layout, no LISTST or SECTRAN).

0.name: BOOT
0.comment: Cold-boot entry — initial system entry at power-on. Loads CCP+BDOS, builds page zero.

3.name: WBOOT
3.comment: Warm-boot entry — reload CCP+BDOS, jump to CCP. Reached via $0000 vector.

6.name: CONST
6.comment: Console status — return A=$00 (no char ready) or A=$FF (char ready).

9.name: CONIN
9.comment: Console input — read one char from console (blocking). Return char in A.

12.name: CONOUT
12.comment: Console output — char in C. Write to console.

15.name: LIST
15.comment: List output — char in C. Write to printer (list device).

18.name: PUNCH
18.comment: Punch output — char in C. Write to paper-tape punch (auxiliary output).

21.name: READER
21.comment: Reader input — read one char from paper-tape reader (auxiliary input). Return in A. EOF = $1A (Ctrl-Z).

24.name: HOME
24.comment: Home — move disk head of current drive to track 0.

27.name: SELDSK
27.comment: Select disk — drive in C ($00=A, $01=B, ...). Return DPH pointer in HL ($0000 if invalid).

30.name: SETTRK
30.comment: Set track — track in BC for next READ/WRITE.

33.name: SETSEC
33.comment: Set sector — sector in BC for next READ/WRITE.

36.name: SETDMA
36.comment: Set DMA address — buffer address in BC for next READ/WRITE.

39.name: READ
39.comment: Read sector — return A=0 success, A!=0 error. Sector data into DMA buffer.

42.name: WRITE
42.comment: Write sector — return A=0 success, A!=0 error.

45.name: LISTST
45.comment: List status (CP/M 2.2 addition) — return A=$FF if ready, A=$00 if busy.

48.name: SECTRAN
48.comment: Sector translate (CP/M 2.2 addition) — logical sector in BC, translation table in DE. Return physical sector in HL.

bdos_functions._description: BDOS function codes passed in register C, invoked via CALL $0005 (BDOS_VEC). Parameters typically in DE; return in A (byte) or HL (word). Functions 30+ are CP/M 2.0/2.2 additions; some are version-specific.

0.name: P_TERMCPM
0.comment: System reset — terminate program, return to CCP. (Equivalent to JP $0000 / CALL WBOOT.)

1.name: C_READ
1.comment: Console input — wait for char, echo it, return in A. Handles Ctrl-S/Q line pause and Ctrl-P printer toggle.

2.name: C_WRITE
2.comment: Console output — char in E. Honors Ctrl-S/Q pause.

3.name: A_READ
3.comment: Auxiliary input (READER) — return char in A.

5.name: L_WRITE
5.comment: List output — char in E. Goes to LIST device.

6.name: C_RAWIO
6.comment: Direct console I/O — E=$FF reads (no echo, no wait), E=$FE returns status, else writes E. CP/M 2.x.

7.name: GET_IOBYTE
7.comment: Get IOBYTE — return $0003 in A. CP/M 2.x; on CP/M 1, function 7 was AUXIN status.

8.name: SET_IOBYTE
8.comment: Set IOBYTE — write E into $0003. CP/M 2.x.

9.name: C_WRITESTR
9.comment: Print '$'-terminated string — DE = string address. Stops at first '$' character.

10.name: C_READSTR
10.comment: Read console buffer — DE = buffer address. Buffer[0]=max len, buffer[1]=actual len, buffer[2..]=chars. Line editing supported.

11.name: C_STAT
11.comment: Console status — A=$00 no char, A=$FF char ready (same as BIOS CONST).

12.name: S_BDOSVER
12.comment: Get BDOS version — return $XXVV in HL. $0022 = CP/M 2.2; H=$00 (CP/M, not MP/M), L=$22 (BCD-ish version).

13.name: DRV_ALLRESET
13.comment: Reset disk system — select A:, set DMA = $0080, mark all drives as logged out.

14.name: DRV_SET
14.comment: Select disk — drive in E ($00=A, $01=B, ..., $0F=P). Logs in if needed.

15.name: F_OPEN
15.comment: Open file — DE = FCB address. A=$FF if not found, A=0..3 = directory entry index if found.

16.name: F_CLOSE
16.comment: Close file — DE = FCB. Updates directory if FCB was modified.

17.name: F_SFIRST
17.comment: Search for first matching directory entry — DE = FCB (with wildcards '?'). A=$FF none, else dir-relative entry index.

18.name: F_SNEXT
18.comment: Search for next matching entry. Continues from F_SFIRST.

19.name: F_DELETE
19.comment: Delete file — DE = FCB. Marks directory entries as deleted ($E5 in drive byte).

20.name: F_READ
20.comment: Read sequential — DE = FCB. Reads next 128-byte record into DMA buffer. A=0 success, $01 EOF, etc.

21.name: F_WRITE
21.comment: Write sequential — DE = FCB. Writes 128 bytes from DMA buffer.

22.name: F_MAKE
22.comment: Make (create) empty file — DE = FCB. A=$FF if directory full, else dir-entry index.

23.name: F_RENAME
23.comment: Rename file — DE = FCB with old name in first half ($00-$0F), new name in second half ($10-$1F).

24.name: DRV_LOGINVEC
24.comment: Login vector — return HL = bit-per-drive map ($0001=A only, $0002=B only, ...). Bit set means logged in.

26.name: F_DMAOFF
26.comment: Set DMA address — DE = address for next READ/WRITE.

27.name: DRV_ALLOCVEC
27.comment: Get allocation vector — HL = pointer to current drive's allocation bitmap.

29.name: DRV_ROVEC
29.comment: Read-only vector — HL = bit-per-drive map of write-protected drives.

30.name: F_ATTRIB
30.comment: Set file attributes — DE = FCB with high bits of name/type bytes set for attributes (R/O, sys, etc.).

31.name: DRV_DPB
31.comment: Get disk parameter block — HL = pointer to current drive's DPB. CP/M 2.x.

32.name: F_USERNUM
32.comment: Get/set user number — E=$FF gets (returns in A), else sets to E (0-15). CP/M 2.x.

33.name: F_READRAND
33.comment: Read random — DE = FCB. Reads record specified by FCB's random-record field. CP/M 2.x.

35.name: F_SIZE
35.comment: Compute file size — DE = FCB. Sets random-record field to file size in records. CP/M 2.x.

36.name: F_RANDREC
36.comment: Set random record — DE = FCB. Computes random-record from current sequential position. CP/M 2.x.

37.name: DRV_RESET
37.comment: Reset specified drives — DE = bit vector of drives to log out. CP/M 2.2.

40.name: F_WRITEZF
40.comment: Write random with zero fill — DE = FCB. CP/M 2.2.

fcb_layout._description: Standard 36-byte File Control Block (FCB) layout. Used by all file BDOS functions. The default FCB at $005C has this structure.

0.name: FCB_DR
0.comment: Drive code: 0=default, 1=A, 2=B, ..., 16=P. $E5 in directory entry = deleted.

1.name: FCB_F1
1.comment: Filename (8 bytes, padded with spaces, ASCII uppercase). High bits used for attributes.

9.name: FCB_T1
9.comment: Filetype (3 bytes, padded with spaces). High bits: T1[7]=R/O, T1[6]=System, T1[5]=Archive.

12.name: FCB_EX
12.comment: Current extent number (low byte, 0-31 for CP/M 2).

14.name: FCB_S2
14.comment: Extent number high byte (CP/M 2.2 supports extents 0-31, with EX=low5, S2=high5 implicitly handling 8 MB max).

16.name: FCB_AL
16.comment: Allocation block numbers — 16 bytes, 8 or 16 entries depending on disk size.

32.name: FCB_CR
32.comment: Current record (0-127) for sequential I/O. Reset to 0 by F_OPEN/F_MAKE.

35.name: FCB_R2
35.comment: Random record number high byte (overflow indicator — only 0 or 1 valid for 8 MB max).

well_known_constants._description: Magic byte values used throughout CP/M.

0xE5.name: DELETED_MARKER
0xE5.comment: $E5 — deleted-file marker (drive byte in directory) and unused-disk-space fill. Also Z-80 PUSH HL opcode (relevant to the v2.20 Videx hang).

0x1A.name: CTRL_Z_EOF
0x1A.comment: $1A (Ctrl-Z) — end-of-file marker for ASCII text files. Used by readers to detect logical EOF before physical EOF.

0x0022.name: VERSION_2_2
0x0022.comment: $0022 — return value of BDOS function 12 (S_BDOSVER) for CP/M 2.2.

memory_model._description: Standard CP/M 2.2 memory layout. Absolute addresses depend on system size (16K, 32K, 48K, 56K, 60K, 64K). Microsoft SoftCard CP/M 2.23 uses the 60K configuration on a 64K Apple ][+ with language card.

tpa_start.comment: First byte of TPA — .COM files load here, execute starting at $0100.

ccp_size.comment: CCP is 2 KB. CCP base = TOP - $1600 (= total memory minus CCP+BDOS+BIOS-area).

bios_size.comment: BIOS is implementation-specific. Microsoft SoftCard 2.23: BIOS at $FAB8 (~1.4 KB to $FFFF).

ccp_base_60k.comment: 60 KB system: CCP cold-start at $E400, warm-start at $E406.

bdos_base_60k.comment: 60 KB system: BDOS entry at $EC00 (BDOS_VEC at $0005 jumps here).

bios_base_60k.comment: 60 KB system: BIOS jump table at $FA00 (typical) or $FAB8 (SoftCard 2.23 specific).
